commit.message:
Initial project setup: vision, roadmap, copilot instructions, and skills

Create foundational documentation and Copilot configuration for
My Little Factory Manager — a deterministic, turn-based deckbuilding
game about fulfilling factory contracts using tool cards.

Deliverables:
- docs/design/vision.md — pure game concept document
- docs/design/roadmap.md — 10-phase implementation plan
- .github/copilot-instructions.md — agent guidance (worktrees, PRs, conventions)
- .github/skills/pre-commit-checks/SKILL.md — commit validation workflow
- .gitignore — Rust ignores + .env protection
- .editorconfig — code style settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: my_little_factory_manager
repository.description: My Little Factory Manager is a deterministic, turn-based deckbuilding game where the player acts as a factory manager fulfilling contracts from an open market. Each contract defines a set of production constraints that must be satisfied using a limited set of tools drawn from a deck.

commit.message:
fix(browser): install chrome-for-testing and fix dubious ownership

playwright-cli@0.1.7 requires chrome-for-testing (not the base image's
bundled Chromium). Add a build step to install it into /ms-playwright.

Also add git safe.directory config to prevent 'dubious ownership' errors
when the container user differs from the clone owner.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add Employee module with separate PostgreSQL schema (#15)

Add WarpBusiness.Employees class library project with:
- Employee entity model with standard HR fields (name, email, hire date,
  department, job title, employment status/type, org hierarchy via ManagerId)
- EmployeeDbContext with HasDefaultSchema("employees") for schema isolation
- EmployeeDbInitializer (IHostedService) for automatic migration on startup
- CRUD DTOs (CreateEmployeeRequest, UpdateEmployeeRequest, EmployeeResponse)
- Minimal API endpoints under /api/employees (list, get, create, update, delete)
- Auto-generated employee numbers (EMP00001, EMP00002, etc.) per tenant
- Tenant-scoped data access via X-Tenant-Id header
- Self-referencing ManagerId FK with SetNull delete behavior
- InitialCreate EF Core migration for the employees schema

Wired into the API project:
- EmployeeDbContext registered on same warpdb connection string
- EmployeeDbInitializer registered as hosted service
- Employee endpoints mapped in Program.cs
- Project added to solution file

- Token stored in localStorage, auto-redirect on 401

feat(pro): add sweep engine, analyze command, and profile generation

Phase 3-4 of Ozone Pro diagnostic suite:

sweep.rs — Smart parameter sweep engine:
- Binary search on gpu_layers to find max that fits VRAM budget
- VRAM pruning via planner::estimate_vram_mb() skips OOM configs
- Early stopping when context level exhausts VRAM budget
- Dominated config detection skips redundant benchmarks
- OOM retry with reduced layers
- Pareto frontier tracking with dominance pruning
- Results stored to SQLite automatically

analyze.rs — Benchmark analysis and auto-profiling:
- show_benchmarks: per-model table or all-models summary
- show_pareto: Pareto frontier with profile labels (speed/balanced/context)
- generate_profiles: auto-generates named presets from frontier
- show_profiles: displays stored profiles per-model or globally

main.rs — New subcommands:
- ozone sweep <model> [--max-context N] [--quick]
- ozone analyze [model] [--all] [--generate] [--profiles]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: auto-locate trtexec in common JetPack paths + auto-install

trtexec is not in PATH on most JetPack installs — it lives at
/usr/src/tensorrt/bin/trtexec. Step 10 now:
1. Checks PATH first
2. Searches common JetPack locations
3. Attempts apt-get install tensorrt as fallback
4. Only skips if all methods fail

The game is the canvas, not the point. The real experiment is observing

Starting rules framed as a deliberately simple starting point.

commit.message:
feat(bench): add ozone bench subcommand

Implements single-model benchmark protocol:
- Clear GPU, launch KoboldCpp, generate, measure, stop
- Measures tokens/sec, TTFT, VRAM peak, RAM peak
- Stores results in SQLite (~/.local/share/ozone/benchmarks.db)
- Uses KoboldCpp /api/extra/perf for accurate token counts
- Handles OOM, timeout, and error cases gracefully

New files:
- src/bench.rs: benchmark protocol (run_benchmark, store_result, print_result)
- src/main.rs: Bench subcommand with --gpu-layers, --context, --quant-kv, --threads

Tested with mn-12b-mag-mell-r1.gguf:
- Full GPU (-1 layers): 48.57 t/s, 8133 MB VRAM
- Mixed (20 layers): 8.15 t/s, 4805 MB VRAM

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: aebrahim <1380168+aebrahim@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add per-session asyncio locks for PTY write serialization

Dashboard WebSocket resize events and MCP tool writes (write_to_pane,
run_in_pane, send_keys, write_raw_bytes) were racing on the same PTY,
producing garbled input like 'RESRe-run s2-posix...IZE:235,59'.

Add zpilot.pty_lock module with per-session asyncio.Lock registry.
All local PTY write paths now acquire the session lock before writing,
making writes atomic. Lock granularity is per-session so different
sessions remain fully concurrent.

Files changed:
- pty_lock.py: new module with get_session_lock/remove_session_lock
- ops.py: lock in write_to_pane, run_in_pane, send_keys, write_raw_bytes
- mcp_http.py: lock in /ws/pty WebSocket recv loop (resize + input)
- web/app.py: lock in /api/send, /api/keys, /api/resize, /ws/terminal,
  /ws/pty WebSocket handlers

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(init): extract make_temp_dir helper to cover cleanup-guard True branch

The seven integration tests in `init.rs` each contained an inline
cleanup guard:

    if tmp.exists() { let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&tmp); }

Because the temporary directory never exists at the start of a fresh CI
run, the True branch (remove existing dir) was never taken.  This left
init.rs at 85.42% branch coverage (7 missed branches out of 48).

The fix follows the established pattern already used in
`workspace_init/mod.rs`:

1. Extract a `make_temp_dir(name)` helper that encapsulates the
   cleanup-before-recreate logic in one place.
2. Add a `make_temp_dir_cleans_up_existing_directory` test that
   pre-creates the directory, then calls `make_temp_dir`, verifying
   that the old tree is removed.  This covers the True branch of the
   helper's cleanup guard.
3. Refactor all seven affected tests to call `make_temp_dir` instead
   of repeating the inline pattern.

Result: init.rs branch coverage 85.42% → 100% (48 → 36 branch points,
0 missed).  Workspace total: 94.41% → 94.71%.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Initial commit: ProguardProtect plugin with 10 R8 crash type detections

- Custom Gradle plugin (lib.proguardprotect) that performs post-build analysis
  of APK/AAB artifacts using mapping.txt + DEX inspection
- Detects 8 crash types: reflection class/method/field, Gson serialization,
  enum stripping, callback interface removal, devirtualization, ServiceLoader
- Multi-variant support: registers tasks for all productFlavors × buildTypes
- Dynamic APK/AAB discovery (no hardcoded filenames)
- AAB support via dexdump-based DEX extraction from bundle zip
- Dark-theme HTML report with source code references and fix suggestions
- Demo app with 4 product flavors (free, paid, demo, internal) × release build
- All 10 issues detected from APK and AAB, 0 false positives with fixes applied
- KDoc on all plugin classes, comprehensive README

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Feat/postgres addition (#5)

* Setup for postgres in separate docker container

* Update backend/src/main.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/src/db/schema.sql

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/src/repositories/drivers_repo.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/src/repositories/teams_repo.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update backend/.dockerignore

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Updated schema for postgres

* fix(schema): change predictions.id from SERIAL to VARCHAR for type consistency

- predictions.id is a string UUID in the model and service layer
- schema was using SERIAL (integer) causing type mismatch
- changed to VARCHAR(50) PRIMARY KEY to match string-based ID strategy
- updated CreatePrediction to generate and insert string ID
- fixed GetByUserAndTime() to include id field in SELECT

* refactor(api): map validation errors to HTTP 400 instead of 500

- created ValidationError type in services.errors to distinguish client errors
- wrapped all validation errors in CreatePrediction: wrong driver/team count, duplicates
- updated 5 API handlers to check error type and return appropriate status:
  * validation errors -> 400 Bad Request (client can fix input)
  * other errors -> 500 Internal Server Error (server issue)
- improves API contract clarity for client error handling

* build(docker): remove unused postgresql-client from runtime image

- entrypoint.sh script is not invoked by the runtime image
- docker-compose.yml already handles service readiness via depends_on/healthcheck
- postgresql-client (~20MB) was only needed for pg_isready in unused script
- reduces image size significantly with no lost functionality
- schema is already copied to runtime stage, DB connections handled natively

* config(ui): make API base URL configurable for multi-environment deployment

- replaced hardcoded localhost:8080 with environment variable support
- web: created getApiUrl() with 3-tier fallback (env var, relative path, localhost)
- mobile: all 3 screens now use REACT_APP_API_BASE_URL env var
- created .env.example template documenting configuration
- supports local dev, Docker deployment, and production environments
- enables proper deployment without code changes

* fix(database): remove COALESCE defeating NULL handling in teams query

- GetTeamWithPositions was using COALESCE(column, 0) to convert NULLs to 0
- COALESCE was converting NULLs before reaching sql.NullInt32 type
- set sql.NullInt32.Valid always true, breaking nil checks
- removed COALESCE to let NULL values flow through properly
- sql.NullInt32.Valid now correctly reflects whether position was set

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(browser): remove PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION from daemon env to fix ENOENT

The playwright-core startDaemon() function passes PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION
as --endpoint to the daemon child process, which interprets 'delegate'
as a browser WebSocket endpoint to connect to — causing 'connect ENOENT
delegate'. Fix: strip the env var and pass session name via -s flag.

Also set cwd to COPILOT_WORK_DIR for consistent workspace detection
(matching copilot dispatch), and make git set-url non-fatal.

commit.message:
fix: replace Unicode chars with ASCII; add UTF-8 BOM to post_install.ps1

PS 5.1 reads .ps1 files as ANSI (system codepage) when no BOM is present.
The em dash (U+2014, UTF-8: E2 80 94) was being misread: byte 0x94 maps to
U+201D (curly right-quote) in cp1252, which unbalances string delimiters and
causes 'Missing closing }' parse errors at runtime on the target machine.

Changes:
- Replace all U+2192 (arrow) with ASCII ->
- Replace all U+2014 (em dash) with ASCII --
- Write file with UTF-8 BOM so PS 5.1 reads it as UTF-8

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: switch to uv/pnpm, all config via frontend UI

- Backend: use uv with pyproject.toml instead of pip + requirements.txt
- Frontend: use pnpm instead of npm

- Only SECRET_KEY and ADMIN_PASSWORD remain as env vars

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: handle orphaned Keycloak users during user creation (#14)

When Keycloak returns 409 Conflict (user exists), the CreateUser endpoint
now looks up the existing Keycloak user by email and checks whether they
exist in the local warp.Users table. If the user is only in Keycloak
(orphaned from a previous partial creation), the endpoint adopts them by
creating the local DB record with the existing Keycloak subject ID,
including UserTenantMembership if a TenantId was provided. If the user
already exists in both systems, the actual duplicate error is returned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: validation uses argmax consistency instead of strict tolerance

Float16 TFLite quantization can exceed 1e-2 atol on certain models
while still predicting the correct class. The validator now uses a
tri-state result:
  pass — values within tolerance
  warn — values differ but predicted class (argmax) matches
  fail — predicted class differs (real problem)

Pipeline only fails if predicted classes actually differ. Float16
precision loss with matching argmax is logged as warning, not failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect WiX bundle entries in pre-clean; delete CPython-3.14 dependency key

Root cause of 'action: Modify' mode:
- Python 3.14.2 (64-bit) GUID {0ef3959c...} is a WiX Burn bundle ARP entry
- msiexec /x returns 1605 on bundles (not an MSI product) which was excluded
  from force-deletion, so the ARP key survived every pre-clean run
- Burn finds this ARP key on next launch -> enters Modify mode
- In Modify mode BA only requests core+exe, not lib_AllUsers -> no stdlib

Fix:
- Detect bundle entries by presence of BundleVersion registry value
- Skip msiexec for bundles; directly Remove-Item the ARP key
- After registry sweep, also delete the Burn dependency provider key
  HKLM/HKCU ..\Installer\Dependencies\CPython-3.14 which is the actual
  trigger for Burn's 'bundle is installed' detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nelson-arbelaez-epam/todos/sessions/c0a58ca3-7dae-4b96-a5f4-d1dc6691e8f1

* fix(mobile): align register flow env and test setup

* fix(mobile): simplify RegisterScreen as pure form presenter

Remove the in-screen success branch and onNavigateToLogin prop so
RegisterScreen is a pure form presenter. Post-registration redirect
is now owned by the Expo Router layer, mirroring web's Register page
pattern and ADR-0024 session-state rules.

* chore: exclude Expo auto-generated files from Biome checks

.expo/ and expo-env.d.ts are generated by the Expo CLI on every
start/build. They carry a 'do not edit' notice and must not be
reformatted by Biome.

* test(mobile): configure vitest alias for expo-router routes

* test(mobile): add unit tests for register flow components and routes

- RegisterForm: validation (empty email, short password) and submit behavior
- RegisterScreen: store wiring for isLoading, error, and register action
- Home route (/): redirect to /register when no session exists
- Register route (/register): redirect to / when already authenticated

* Require mobile API base URL

Fail fast in the mobile auth client when EXPO_PUBLIC_TODOS_API_URL is missing and align the documented default API port with the current local contract.

* Clean up MCP README boilerplate

Remove stale NestJS template reference lines and normalize the install snippet without mixing that cleanup into the mobile env-contract change.

* Pin Vitest versions across workspaces

Replace floating and divergent Vitest ranges with a single explicit workspace version so installs and lockfile refreshes stay deterministic.

---------

feat: SandCastle Wars v2 golden master

Simplified game rules reset for a fresh experiment:
- Single block type: sand (50 HP)
- 3 actions: PLACE, REMOVE, REINFORCE
- 20 actions per tick, multi-level building (L0-L3)
- 5 weather events with real Sydney weather data
- Visual LLM judge, flags, water zone
- Full UI: isometric/top-down views, god mode, timeline scrubber
- Game improvement pipeline (suggest → review → implement → auto-merge)
- Player repo templates + bootstrap.sh for automated setup
- 167 tests passing across 8 test files
